questions.text: What is the difference between the Requirements-First and Design-First flows in Feature Specs?

options.label: Requirements-First starts with requirements; Design-First starts with technical architecture
Requirements-First is for small projects; Design-First is for large projects
Requirements-First generates tests before code; Design-First generates code before tests
There is no difference, they are alternative names for the same flow

questions.explanation: In the Requirements-First flow, you define requirements and acceptance criteria before the design. In Design-First, you start with the technical architecture and then derive requirements from it.

questions.text: In a Feature Spec, the design document should include correctness properties that can be verified by property-based tests.
questions.explanation: During the design phase of a Feature Spec, Kiro extracts correctness properties from EARS requirements. These properties define universal behaviors verifiable by property-based testing, serving as a bridge between human-readable specifications and machine-testable guarantees.

questions.text: You are creating a Feature Spec and Kiro detected ambiguities in the requirements. What happens?

options.label: Kiro presents clarifying questions and suggests automatic resolutions when possible
Kiro resolves all ambiguities on its own, making assumptions, and proceeds to design without warning
Kiro marks the ambiguous requirements as complete and excludes them from the final design
Kiro blocks the spec until you manually rewrite all requirements from scratch

questions.explanation: When Kiro detects ambiguities in requirements, it uses the reasoning service to present clarifying questions. Self-resolvable items are included with recommended answers, while remaining questions are presented to the user.

questions.text: Arrange the steps of a Feature Spec workflow in the order they occur:

options.label: Define requirements with user stories and acceptance criteria
Create design document with architecture and components
Generate plan of discrete and trackable tasks
Execute tasks individually or in parallel

questions.explanation: The Feature Spec workflow follows three documented phases: Requirements (define requirements and acceptance criteria), Design (create architecture and technical decisions in design.md), and Tasks (generate discrete tasks in tasks.md). Task execution concludes the cycle with verifiable implementation.
